lede: I'm a software engineer working at the seams where intent, data, and interface disagree. A decade on the front end is the proof of craft, not the caveat.
how: My work follows a small pipeline: shape, research, write, execute, compound. Each feature leaves the system smarter than it found it. I optimize for correctness and stability first; explicit state, clear data flow, minimal magic.
where: Bloomington, Indiana. Remote-first by choice — the work is portable, and this is where it gets done well.
objects: Field notebook whose first page is an index
Plex Mono on a tactile keyboard
One small plant that has survived three moves
